metadata.title: Characterizing, Modeling, and Benchmarking RocksDB Key-Value Workloads at Facebook
metadata.titleTranslation: Facebook 的 RocksDB 键值工作负载：特征分析、建模与基准测试
metadata.venue: 18th USENIX Conference on File and Storage Technologies (FAST '20)

sections.title: Abstract
sections.originalMarkdown:
Persistent key-value stores are widely used as building blocks in today’s IT infrastructure for managing and storing large amounts of data. However, studies of characterizing real-world workloads for key-value stores are limited due to the lack of tracing/analyzing tools and the difficulty of collecting traces in operational environments. In this paper, we first present a detailed characterization of workloads from three typical RocksDB production use cases at Facebook: UDB (a MySQL storage layer for social graph data), ZippyDB (a distributed key-value store), and UP2X (a distributed key-value store for AI/ML services). These characterizations reveal several interesting findings: first, that the distribution of key and value sizes are highly related to the use cases/applications; second, that the accesses to key-value pairs have a good locality and follow certain special patterns; and third, that the collected performance metrics show a strong diurnal pattern in the UDB, but not the other two.

We further discover that although the widely used key-value benchmark YCSB provides various workload configurations and key-value pair access distribution models, the YCSB-triggered workloads for underlying storage systems are still not close enough to the workloads we collected due to ignorance of key-space localities. To address this issue, we propose a key-range based modeling and develop a benchmark that can better emulate the workloads of real-world key-value stores. This benchmark can synthetically generate more precise key-value queries that represent the reads and writes of key-value stores to the underlying storage system.
sections.translationMarkdown:
持久化键值存储被广泛用作当今 IT 基础设施的构建模块，用于管理和存储海量数据。然而，由于缺少跟踪/分析工具，并且难以在生产运行环境中收集跟踪数据，针对键值存储真实工作负载特征的研究仍然有限。本文首先对 Facebook 中三种典型的 RocksDB 生产用例的工作负载进行了详细刻画：UDB（面向社交图数据的 MySQL 存储层）、ZippyDB（分布式键值存储）和 UP2X（面向 AI/ML 服务的分布式键值存储）。这些特征分析揭示了若干有趣的发现：第一，键和值的大小分布与具体用例/应用高度相关；第二，对键值对的访问具有良好的局部性，并遵循某些特殊模式；第三，在 UDB 中，收集到的性能指标呈现出明显的昼夜模式，而另外两个用例中则没有。

我们进一步发现，虽然广泛使用的键值基准测试 YCSB 提供了多种工作负载配置和键值对访问分布模型，但由于忽略了键空间局部性，YCSB 在底层存储系统上触发的工作负载与我们收集到的工作负载仍不够接近。为解决这一问题，我们提出一种基于键范围的建模方法，并开发了一个能够更好模拟真实键值存储工作负载的基准测试。该基准测试可以合成生成更精确的键值查询，以表征键值存储对底层存储系统的读写。

sections.title: 1 Introduction
sections.originalMarkdown:
In current IT infrastructure, persistent key-value stores (KV-stores) are widely used as storage engines to support various upper-layer applications. The high performance, flexibility, and ease of use of KV-stores have attracted more users and developers. Many existing systems and applications like file systems, object-based storage systems, SQL databases, and even AI/ML systems use KV-stores as backend storage to achieve high performance and high space efficiency [10,16,28,36].

However, tuning and improving the performance of KV-stores is still challenging. First, there are very limited studies of real-world workload characterization and analysis for KV-stores, and the performance of KV-stores is highly related to the workloads generated by applications. Second, the analytic methods for characterizing KV-store workloads are different from the existing workload characterization studies for block storage or file systems. KV-stores have simple but very different interfaces and behaviors. A set of good workload collection, analysis, and characterization tools can benefit both developers and users of KV-stores by optimizing performance and developing new functions. Third, when evaluating underlying storage systems of KV-stores, it is unknown whether the workloads generated by KV-store benchmarks are representative of real-world KV-store workloads.

To address these issues, in this paper, we characterize, model, and benchmark workloads of RocksDB (a high-performance persistent KV-store) at Facebook. To our knowledge, this is the first study that characterizes persistent KV-store workloads. First, we introduce a set of tools that can be used in production to collect the KV-level query traces, replay the traces, and analyze the traces. These tools are open-sourced in RocksDB release [20] and are used within Facebook for debugging and performance tuning KV-stores. Second, to achieve a better understanding of the KV workloads and their correlations to the applications, we select three RocksDB use cases at Facebook to study: 1) UDB, 2) ZippyDB, and 3) UP2X. These three use cases are typical examples of how KV-stores are used: 1) as the storage engine of a SQL database, 2) as the storage engine of a distributed KV-store, and 3) as the persistent storage for artificial-intelligence/machine-learning (AI/ML) services.

UDB is the MySQL storage layer for social graph data at Facebook, and RocksDB is used as its backend storage engine. Social graph data is maintained in the MySQL tables, and table rows are stored as KV-pairs in RocksDB. The conversion from MySQL tables to RocksDB KV-pairs is achieved by MyRocks [19,36]. ZippyDB is a distributed KV-store that uses RocksDB as the storage nodes to achieve data persistency and reliability. ZippyDB usually stores data like photo metadata and the metadata of objects in storage. In this paper, the workloads of ZippyDB were collected from shards that store the metadata of an object storage system at Facebook (called ObjStorage in this paper). The key usually contains the metadata of an ObjStorage file or a data block, and the value stores the corresponding object address. UP2X is a special distributed KV-store based on RocksDB. UP2X stores the profile data (e.g., counters and statistics) used for the prediction and inferencing of several AI/ML services at Facebook. Therefore, the KV-pairs in UP2X are frequently updated.

Based on a set of collected workloads, we further explore the specific characteristics of KV-stores. From our analyses, we find that 1) read dominates the queries in UDB and ZippyDB, while read-modify-write (Merge) is the major query type in UP2X; 2) key sizes are usually small and have a narrow distribution due to the key composition design from upper-layer applications, and large value sizes only appear in some special cases; 3) most KV-pairs are cold (less accessed), and only a small portion of KV-pairs are frequently accessed; 4) Get, Put, and Iterator have strong key-space localities (e.g., frequently accessed KV-pairs are within relatively close locations in the key-space), and some key-ranges that are closely related to the request localities of upper-layer applications are extremely hot (frequently accessed); and 5) the accesses in UDB explicitly exhibit a diurnal pattern, unlike those in ZippyDB and UP2X, which do not show such a clear pattern.

Benchmarks are widely used to evaluate KV-store performance and to test underlying storage systems. With real-world traces, we investigate whether the existing KV benchmarks can synthetically generate real-world-like workloads with storage I/Os that display similar characteristics. YCSB [11] is one of the most widely used KV benchmarks and has become the gold standard of KV-store benchmarking. It provides different workload models, various query types, flexible configurations, and supports most of the widely used KV-stores. YCSB can help users simulate real-world workloads in a convenient way. However, we find that even though YCSB can generate workloads that have similar key-value (KV) query statistics as shown in ZippyDB workloads, the RocksDB storage I/Os can be quite different. This issue is mainly caused by the fact that the YCSB-generated workloads ignore key-space localities. In YCSB, hot KV-pairs are either randomly allocated across the whole key-space or clustered together. This results in an I/O mismatch between accessed data blocks in storage and the data blocks associated with KV queries. Without considering key-space localities, a benchmark will generate workloads that cause RocksDB to have a bigger read amplification and a smaller write amplification than those in real-world workloads.

To develop a benchmark that can more precisely emulate KV-store workloads, we propose a workload modeling method based on the hotness of key-ranges. The whole key-space is partitioned into small key-ranges, and we model the hotness of these small key-ranges. In the new benchmark, queries are assigned to key-ranges based on the distribution of key-range hotness, and hot keys are allocated closely in each key-range. In our evaluation, under the same configuration, YCSB causes at least 500% more read-bytes and delivers only 17% of the cache hits in RocksDB compared with real-world workloads. The workloads generated by our proposed new benchmark have only 43% more read-bytes and achieve about 77% of the cache hits in RocksDB, and thus are much closer to real-world workloads. Moreover, we use UDB as an example to show that the synthetic workloads generated by the new benchmark have a good fit of the distributions in key/value sizes, KV-pair accesses, and Iterator scan lengths.

This paper is organized as follows. First, we introduce RocksDB and the system background of three RocksDB use cases in Section 2. We describe our methodology and tools in Section 3. The detailed workload characteristics of the three use cases including the general query statistics, key and value sizes, and KV-pair access distributions are presented in 4, 5, and 6 respectively. In Section 7, we present the investigation results of the storage statistics of YCSB, and describe the proposed new modeling and benchmarking methods. We also compare the results of YCSB with those of the new benchmark. Related work is described in Section 8, and we conclude the paper in Section 9.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在当前 IT 基础设施中，持久化键值存储（KV-store）被广泛用作存储引擎，以支持各种上层应用。键值存储的高性能、灵活性和易用性吸引了越来越多的用户和开发者。文件系统、基于对象的存储系统、SQL 数据库，乃至 AI/ML 系统等许多现有系统和应用，都使用键值存储作为后端存储，以实现高性能和较高的空间效率 [10,16,28,36]。

然而，调优和提升键值存储的性能仍然颇具挑战。第一，针对键值存储真实工作负载的特征刻画与分析研究非常有限，而键值存储的性能又与应用所产生的工作负载高度相关。第二，用于刻画键值存储工作负载的分析方法，不同于已有的块存储或文件系统工作负载特征研究。键值存储的接口虽然简单，但其接口和行为与前述系统存在很大差异。一套良好的工作负载收集、分析和特征刻画工具，可以通过性能优化和新功能开发，使键值存储的开发者与用户双方受益。第三，在评估键值存储的底层存储系统时，人们尚不清楚键值存储基准测试所生成的工作负载能否代表真实的键值存储工作负载。

为解决这些问题，本文对 Facebook 中 RocksDB（一种高性能持久化键值存储）的工作负载进行特征刻画、建模和基准测试。据我们所知，这是首项对持久化键值存储工作负载进行特征刻画的研究。首先，我们介绍了一套可用于生产环境的工具，用来收集键值级查询跟踪、重放跟踪并分析跟踪。这些工具已在 RocksDB 发行版 [20] 中开源，并在 Facebook 内部用于键值存储的调试和性能调优。其次，为了更深入地理解键值工作负载及其与应用的相关性，我们选择了 Facebook 中三种 RocksDB 用例进行研究：1）UDB；2）ZippyDB；3）UP2X。这三个用例是键值存储使用方式的典型示例：1）作为 SQL 数据库的存储引擎；2）作为分布式键值存储的存储引擎；3）作为人工智能/机器学习（AI/ML）服务的持久化存储。

UDB 是 Facebook 面向社交图数据的 MySQL 存储层，RocksDB 被用作其后端存储引擎。社交图数据维护在 MySQL 表中，而表中的行则以键值对的形式存储在 RocksDB 中。MySQL 表到 RocksDB 键值对的转换由 MyRocks 实现 [19,36]。ZippyDB 是一种分布式键值存储，它使用 RocksDB 作为存储节点，以实现数据持久性和可靠性。ZippyDB 通常存储照片元数据以及存储中对象的元数据等数据。本文中的 ZippyDB 工作负载采集自用于存储 Facebook 某对象存储系统元数据的分片（本文称该系统为 ObjStorage）。键通常包含 ObjStorage 文件或数据块的元数据，值则存储相应的对象地址。UP2X 是一种基于 RocksDB 的特殊分布式键值存储。UP2X 存储 Facebook 多种 AI/ML 服务在预测和推理过程中使用的画像数据（例如计数器和统计数据）。因此，UP2X 中的键值对会被频繁更新。

基于一组收集到的工作负载，我们进一步探索了键值存储的具体特征。通过分析，我们发现：1）在 UDB 和 ZippyDB 中，读操作在查询中占主导，而在 UP2X 中，读-修改-写（Merge）是主要查询类型；2）由于上层应用对键的组成方式进行了设计，键通常较小且分布范围较窄，较大的值只会出现在某些特殊情形中；3）大多数键值对都是冷的（访问较少），只有一小部分键值对会被频繁访问；4）Get、Put 和 Iterator 都具有很强的键空间局部性（例如，频繁访问的键值对在键空间中的位置相对接近），而且某些与上层应用请求局部性密切相关的键范围会极热（被频繁访问）；5）UDB 中的访问显著呈现昼夜模式，而 ZippyDB 和 UP2X 中没有如此清晰的模式。

基准测试被广泛用于评估键值存储的性能和测试底层存储系统。借助真实跟踪数据，我们考察现有键值基准测试能否合成生成类似真实情况的工作负载，并使其存储 I/O 呈现相似特征。YCSB [11] 是使用最广泛的键值基准测试之一，已经成为键值存储基准测试的黄金标准。它提供不同的工作负载模型、多种查询类型和灵活的配置，并支持大多数被广泛使用的键值存储。YCSB 可以帮助用户便捷地模拟真实工作负载。然而，我们发现，即使 YCSB 能够生成键值查询统计特征与 ZippyDB 工作负载相近的工作负载，RocksDB 的存储 I/O 仍可能存在很大差异。造成这一问题的主要原因是 YCSB 生成的工作负载忽略了键空间局部性。在 YCSB 中，热键值对要么随机分配在整个键空间中，要么聚集在一起。这会导致存储中被访问的数据块与键值查询所关联的数据块之间出现 I/O 不匹配。如果不考虑键空间局部性，基准测试生成的工作负载会使 RocksDB 的读放大大于真实工作负载，而写放大小于真实工作负载。

为了开发一个能够更精确模拟键值存储工作负载的基准测试，我们提出了一种基于键范围热度的工作负载建模方法。整个键空间被划分为若干较小的键范围，我们对这些小键范围的热度进行建模。在新的基准测试中，查询依据键范围热度的分布被分配到各个键范围，而热键则被紧密地分配在每个键范围内。在我们的评估中，在相同配置下，与真实工作负载相比，YCSB 导致的读取字节数至少多出 500%，而其在 RocksDB 中产生的缓存命中数仅为真实工作负载的 17%。我们所提出的新基准测试生成的工作负载仅多出 43% 的读取字节数，并在 RocksDB 中达到真实工作负载约 77% 的缓存命中数，因此与真实工作负载更加接近。此外，我们以 UDB 为例说明，新基准测试生成的合成工作负载能够良好拟合键/值大小、键值对访问次数以及 Iterator 扫描长度的分布。

本文其余部分组织如下。首先，我们在第 2 节介绍 RocksDB 以及三种 RocksDB 用例的系统背景。第 3 节介绍我们的方法和工具。第 4、5、6 节分别给出三个用例的详细工作负载特征，包括一般查询统计信息、键和值的大小，以及键值对访问分布。第 7 节给出对 YCSB 存储统计信息的考察结果，并介绍所提出的新建模和基准测试方法。我们还比较了 YCSB 与新基准测试的结果。第 8 节介绍相关工作，第 9 节对全文进行总结。

sections.title: 2 Background
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section, we first briefly introduce KV-stores and RocksDB. Then, we provide background on three RocksDB use cases at Facebook, UDB, ZippyDB, and UP2X, to promote understanding of their workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节首先简要介绍键值存储和 RocksDB。随后，我们介绍 Facebook 中 UDB、ZippyDB 和 UP2X 这三种 RocksDB 用例的背景，以帮助理解它们的工作负载。

sections.title: 2.1 Key-Value Stores and RocksDB
sections.originalMarkdown:
KV-store is a type of data storage that stores and accesses data based on {key, value} pairs. A key uniquely identifies the KV-pair, and the value holds the data. KV-stores are widely used by companies as distributed hash tables (e.g., Amazon Dynamo [14]), in-memory databases (e.g., Redis [39]), and persistent storage (e.g., BigTable [8] from Google and RocksDB [15,21] from Facebook).

RocksDB is a high-performance embedded persistent KV-store that was derived from LevelDB [23] by Facebook [15,21] and was optimized for fast storage devices such as Solid State Drives. RocksDB is also used by many large websites, like Alibaba [44], Yahoo [37], and LinkedIn [24]. At Facebook, RocksDB is used as the storage engine for several data storage services, such as MySQL [19,36], Laser [9], Cassandra [16], ZippyDB [1], and AI/ML platforms.

RocksDB supports KV interfaces like Get, Put, Delete, Iterator (scan), SingleDelete, DeleteRange, and Merge. Get, Put, and Delete are used to read, write, and delete a KV-pair with certain key respectively. Iterator is used to scan a set of consecutive KV-pairs beginning with a start-key. The scan direction can be either forward (calling Nexts) or backward (calling Prevs). SingleDelete can only be used to delete a KV-pair that has not been overwritten [22]. DeleteRange is used to delete a range of keys between [start, end) (the end-key is excluded from the deletion). RocksDB encapsulates the semantics for read-modify-write into a simple abstract interface called Merge [17], which avoids the performance overhead of a random Get before every Put. Merge stores the delta of the write to RocksDB, and these deltas can be stacked or already combined. This incurs a high read overhead because a Get to one key requires finding and combining all the previously stored deltas with the same key inserted by a Merge. The combine function is defined by users as a RocksDB plugin.

*Figure 1: The basic architecture of RocksDB.*

RocksDB adopts a Log-Structured Merge-Tree [38] to maintain the KV-pairs in persistent storage (e.g., file systems). The basic architecture of RocksDB is shown in Figure 1. One RocksDB maintains at least one logical partition called Column Family (CF), which has its own in-memory write buffer (Memtable). When a Memtable is full, it is flushed to the file system and stored as a Sorted Sequence Table (SST) file. SST files persistently store the KV-pairs in a sorted fashion and are organized in a sequence of levels starting from Level-0. When one level reaches its limit, one SST file is selected to be merged with the SST files in the next level that have overlapping key-ranges, which is called compaction. Detailed information about RocksDB is described in [15,21]
sections.translationMarkdown:
键值存储是一类以 {key, value} 对为基础存储和访问数据的数据存储。键唯一标识一个键值对，值则保存数据。企业广泛地将键值存储用作分布式哈希表（例如 Amazon Dynamo [14]）、内存数据库（例如 Redis [39]）和持久化存储（例如 Google 的 BigTable [8] 和 Facebook 的 RocksDB [15,21]）。

RocksDB 是 Facebook 从 LevelDB [23] 衍生而来的一种高性能嵌入式持久化键值存储 [15,21]，并针对固态硬盘等高速存储设备进行了优化。RocksDB 也被许多大型网站采用，例如 Alibaba [44]、Yahoo [37] 和 LinkedIn [24]。在 Facebook，RocksDB 被用作多种数据存储服务的存储引擎，例如 MySQL [19,36]、Laser [9]、Cassandra [16]、ZippyDB [1] 以及 AI/ML 平台。

RocksDB 支持 Get、Put、Delete、Iterator（扫描）、SingleDelete、DeleteRange 和 Merge 等键值接口。Get、Put 和 Delete 分别用于读取、写入和删除具有特定键的键值对。Iterator 用于扫描从起始键开始的一组连续键值对。扫描方向可以向前（调用 Next）或向后（调用 Prev）。SingleDelete 只能用于删除尚未被覆盖写过的键值对 [22]。DeleteRange 用于删除 [start, end) 之间的一段键范围（删除范围不包括结束键）。RocksDB 将读-修改-写的语义封装为一个称作 Merge [17] 的简单抽象接口，从而避免每次 Put 之前执行随机 Get 所带来的性能开销。Merge 将写入的增量存储到 RocksDB 中，这些增量可以堆叠起来，也可以已经合并。这会带来很高的读取开销，因为对某个键执行 Get 时，需要找出由 Merge 插入的、此前存储的所有同键增量并将它们合并。合并函数由用户以 RocksDB 插件的形式定义。

*图 1：RocksDB 的基本架构。*

RocksDB 采用日志结构合并树（Log-Structured Merge-Tree）[38] 来维护持久化存储（例如文件系统）中的键值对。RocksDB 的基本架构如图 1 所示。一个 RocksDB 至少维护一个称为列族（Column Family，CF）的逻辑分区，每个列族都有自己的内存写缓冲区（Memtable）。当 Memtable 写满时，它会被刷写到文件系统，并以排序序列表（Sorted Sequence Table，SST）文件的形式存储。SST 文件以有序方式持久保存键值对，并被组织为从 Level-0 开始的一系列层级。当某一层达到其容量限制时，会选择其中一个 SST 文件，与下一层中键范围与其重叠的 SST 文件进行合并，这一过程称为压实（compaction）。关于 RocksDB 的详细信息见文献 [15,21]。

sections.title: 2.2 Background of Three RocksDB Use Cases
sections.titleTranslation: 2.2 三种 RocksDB 用例的背景
sections.originalMarkdown:
We discuss three important and large-scale production use cases of RocksDB at Facebook: 1) UDB; 2) ZippyDB; and 3) UP2X. Sharding is used in all three use cases to achieve load balancing. Therefore, the workloads are very similar among all shards, and we randomly select three RocksDB instances from each use case to collect the traces.

**UDB:** Social graph data at Facebook is persistently stored in UDB, a sharded MySQL database tier [4]. The cache read misses and all writes to social graph data are processed by UDB servers. UDB relies on the MySQL instance to handle all queries, and these queries are converted to RocksDB queries via MyRocks [19,36]. Much of the social graph data is presented as objects and associations, and is maintained in different MySQL tables following the model introduced in [4]. RocksDB uses different Column Families (CFs) to store object- and association-related data.

There are 6 major CFs in UDB: Object, Assoc, Assoc_count, Object_2ry, Assoc_2ry, and Non_SG. Object stores social graph object data and Assoc stores social graph association data, which defines connections between two objects. Assoc_count stores the number of associations of each object. Association counters are always updated with new values and do not have any deletions. Object_2ry and Assoc_2ry are the CFs that maintain the secondary indexes of objects and associations, respectively. They are also used for the purpose of ETL (Extract, Transform, and Load data from databases). Non_SG stores data from other non-social graph related services.

Because the UDB workload is an example of KV queries converted from SQL queries, some special patterns exist. We collected the traces for 14 days. Since the workload characteristics of three UDB servers are very similar, we present only one of them. The total trace file size in this server is about 1.1 TB. For some characteristics, daily data is more important. Thus, we also analyzed the workload of the last day in the 14-day period (24-hour trace) separately.

**ZippyDB:** A high-performance distributed KV-store called ZippyDB was developed based on RocksDB and relies on Paxos [29] to achieve data consistency and reliability. KV-pairs are divided into shards, and each shard is supported by one RocksDB instance. One of the replicas is selected as the primary shard, and the others are secondary. The primary shard processes all the writes to a certain shard. If strong consistency is required for reads, read requests (e.g., Get and Scan) are only processed by the primary shard. One ZippyDB query is converted to a set of RocksDB queries (one or more).

Compared with the UDB use case, the upper-layer queries in ZippyDB are directly mapped to the RocksDB queries, and so the workload characteristics of ZippyDB are very different. We randomly selected three primary shards of ZippyDB and collected the traces for 24 hours. Like UDB, we present only one of them. This shard stores the metadata of ObjStorage, which is an object storage system at Facebook. In this shard, a KV-pair usually contains the metadata information for an ObjStorage file or a data block with its address information.

**UP2X:** Facebook uses various AI/ML services to support social networks, and a huge number of dynamically changing data sets (e.g., the statistic counters of user activities) are used for AI/ML prediction and inferencing. UP2X is a distributed KV-store that was developed specifically to store this type of data as KV-pairs. As users use Facebook services, the KV-pairs in UP2X are frequently updated, such as when counters increase. If UP2X called Get before each Put to achieve a read-modify-write operation, it would have a high overhead due to the relatively slow speed of random Gets. UP2X leverages the RocksDB Merge interface to avoid Gets during the updates.

KV-pairs in UP2X are divided into shards supported by RocksDB instances. We randomly selected three RocksDB instances from UP2X and then collected and analyzed the 24-hour traces. Note that the KV-pairs inserted by Merge are cleaned during compaction via Compaction Filter, which uses custom logic to delete or modify KV-pairs in the background during compaction. Therefore, a large number of KV-pairs are removed from UP2X even though the delete operations (e.g., Delete, DeleteRange, and SingleDelete) are not used.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们讨论 Facebook 中 RocksDB 的三种重要大规模生产用例：1）UDB；2）ZippyDB；3）UP2X。这三种用例都采用分片实现负载均衡。因此，各分片之间的工作负载非常相似，我们从每种用例中随机选择三个 RocksDB 实例来收集跟踪数据。

**UDB：**Facebook 的社交图数据持久存储在 UDB 中；UDB 是一个采用分片的 MySQL 数据库层 [4]。社交图数据的缓存读未命中请求和所有写请求均由 UDB 服务器处理。UDB 依赖 MySQL 实例处理所有查询，这些查询通过 MyRocks 被转换为 RocksDB 查询 [19,36]。大量社交图数据以对象和关联的形式表示，并按照文献 [4] 介绍的模型维护在不同的 MySQL 表中。RocksDB 使用不同的列族（CF）存储与对象及关联有关的数据。

UDB 中有 6 个主要 CF：Object、Assoc、Assoc_count、Object_2ry、Assoc_2ry 和 Non_SG。Object 存储社交图对象数据，Assoc 存储社交图关联数据，后者定义两个对象之间的连接。Assoc_count 存储每个对象的关联数量。关联计数器总是以新值更新，从不执行删除。Object_2ry 和 Assoc_2ry 分别是维护对象和关联二级索引的 CF。它们也用于 ETL（从数据库中提取、转换和加载数据）。Non_SG 存储来自其他非社交图相关服务的数据。

由于 UDB 工作负载是由 SQL 查询转换为键值查询的一个实例，因此其中存在一些特殊模式。我们收集了持续 14 天的跟踪数据。由于三台 UDB 服务器的工作负载特征非常相似，因此只展示其中一台。该服务器上的跟踪文件总大小约为 1.1 TB。对于某些特征而言，每日数据更为重要。因此，我们还单独分析了这 14 天中最后一天的工作负载（24 小时跟踪数据）。

**ZippyDB：**ZippyDB 是一种基于 RocksDB 开发的高性能分布式键值存储，依靠 Paxos [29] 实现数据一致性和可靠性。键值对被划分到不同分片中，每个分片由一个 RocksDB 实例提供支持。多个副本中的一个被选为主分片，其余则作为从分片。主分片处理发往某一分片的所有写入。如果读取要求强一致性，则读请求（例如 Get 和 Scan）只能由主分片处理。一条 ZippyDB 查询会被转换为一组 RocksDB 查询（一个或多个）。

与 UDB 用例相比，ZippyDB 中的上层查询会直接映射为 RocksDB 查询，因此 ZippyDB 的工作负载特征有很大不同。我们随机选择了 ZippyDB 的三个主分片，并收集了持续 24 小时的跟踪数据。与 UDB 一样，我们只展示其中一个分片。该分片存储 ObjStorage 的元数据，ObjStorage 是 Facebook 的一个对象存储系统。在该分片中，一个键值对通常包含某个 ObjStorage 文件或数据块的元数据信息及其地址信息。

**UP2X：**Facebook 使用多种 AI/ML 服务来支撑社交网络，并利用数量庞大且动态变化的数据集（例如用户活动的统计计数器）进行 AI/ML 预测和推理。UP2X 是一种专门开发的分布式键值存储，用于以键值对的形式存储这类数据。随着用户使用 Facebook 服务，UP2X 中的键值对会被频繁更新，例如计数器递增时。如果 UP2X 为了实现读-修改-写操作而在每次 Put 前调用 Get，那么由于随机 Get 的速度相对较慢，将产生很高的开销。UP2X 利用 RocksDB 的 Merge 接口来避免在更新期间执行 Get。

UP2X 中的键值对被划分到由 RocksDB 实例支持的各个分片中。我们从 UP2X 中随机选择了三个 RocksDB 实例，然后收集并分析了持续 24 小时的跟踪数据。需要注意的是，由 Merge 插入的键值对会在压实期间由 Compaction Filter 清理；Compaction Filter 使用自定义逻辑，在后台压实过程中删除或修改键值对。因此，即使没有使用删除操作（例如 Delete、DeleteRange 和 SingleDelete），UP2X 中仍有大量键值对被移除。

sections.title: 3 Methodology and Tool Set
sections.originalMarkdown:
To analyze and characterize RocksDB workloads from different use cases and to generate synthetic workloads, we propose and develop a set of KV-store tracing, replaying, analyzing, modeling, and benchmarking tools. These tools are already open-sourced in RocksDB release [20]. In this section, we present these tools and discuss how they are used to characterize and generate KV-store workloads.

**Tracing** The tracing tool collects query information at RocksDB public KV interfaces and writes to a trace file as records. It stores the following five types of information in each trace record: 1) query type, 2) CF ID, 3) key, 4) query specific data, and 5) timestamp. For Put and Merge, we store the value information in the query-specific data. For Iterator queries like Seek and SeekForPrev, the scan length (the number of Next or Prev called after Seek or SeekForPrev) is stored in the query-specific data. The timestamp is collected when RocksDB public interfaces are called with microsecond accuracy. In order to log the trace record of each query in a trace file, a lock is used to serialize all the queries, which will potentially incur some performance overhead. However, according to the performance monitoring statistics in production under the regular production workloads, we did not observe major throughput degradation or increased latency caused by the tracing tool.

**Trace Replaying** The collected trace files can be replayed through a Replayer tool implemented in db_bench (special plugins like MergeOperator or Comparator are required if they are used in the original RocksDB instance). The replay tool issues the queries to RocksDB based on the trace record information, and the time intervals between the queries follow the timestamps in the trace. By setting different fast forward and multithreading parameters, RocksDB can be benchmarked with workloads of different intensities. However, query order is not guaranteed with multithreading. The workloads generated by Replayer can be considered as real-world workloads.

**Trace Analyzing** Using collected traces for replaying has its limitations. Due to the potential performance overhead of workload tracing, it is difficult to track large-scale and long-lasting workloads. Moreover, the content of trace files is sensitive and confidential for their users/owners, so it is very hard for RocksDB users to share the traces with other RocksDB developers or developers from third-party companies (e.g., upper-layer application developers or storage vendors) for benchmarking and performance tuning. To address these limitations, we propose a way of analyzing RocksDB workloads that profiles the workloads based on information in the traces. The trace analyzing tool reads a trace file and provides the following characteristics: 1) a detailed statistical summary of the KV-pairs in each CF, query numbers, and query types; 2) key size and value size statistics; 3) KV-pair popularity; 4) the key-space locality, which combines the accessed keys with all existing keys from the database in a sorted order; and 5) Queries Per Second (QPS) statistics.

**Modeling and Benchmarking** We first calculate the Pearson correlation coefficients between any two selected variables to ensure that these variables have very low correlations. In this way, each variable can be modeled separately. Then, we fit the collected workloads to different statistical models to find out which one has the lowest fitting error, which is more accurate than always fitting different workloads to the same model (like Zipfian). The proposed benchmark can then generate KV queries based on these probability models. Details are discussed in Section 7.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了分析并刻画来自不同用例的 RocksDB 工作负载，以及生成合成工作负载，我们提出并开发了一套用于键值存储跟踪、重放、分析、建模和基准测试的工具。这些工具已经在 RocksDB 发行版 [20] 中开源。本节将介绍这些工具，并讨论如何使用它们刻画和生成键值存储工作负载。

**跟踪（Tracing）** 跟踪工具在 RocksDB 的公共键值接口处收集查询信息，并将其作为记录写入跟踪文件。每条跟踪记录存储以下五类信息：1）查询类型；2）CF ID；3）键；4）查询特定数据；5）时间戳。对于 Put 和 Merge，我们在查询特定数据中存储值信息。对于 Seek 和 SeekForPrev 之类的 Iterator 查询，查询特定数据中存储扫描长度（即 Seek 或 SeekForPrev 之后调用 Next 或 Prev 的次数）。时间戳在调用 RocksDB 公共接口时采集，精度为微秒。为了将每条查询的跟踪记录写入跟踪文件，需要使用一把锁来串行化所有查询，这可能会产生一定的性能开销。然而，根据常规生产工作负载下生产环境中的性能监控统计数据，我们没有观察到由跟踪工具造成的显著吞吐量下降或延迟增加。

**跟踪重放（Trace Replaying）** 收集到的跟踪文件可以通过在 db_bench 中实现的 Replayer 工具进行重放（如果原 RocksDB 实例使用了 MergeOperator 或 Comparator 等特殊插件，则重放时也需要这些插件）。重放工具根据跟踪记录中的信息向 RocksDB 发出查询，查询之间的时间间隔遵循跟踪中的时间戳。通过设置不同的快进和多线程参数，可以使用不同强度的工作负载对 RocksDB 进行基准测试。不过，采用多线程时无法保证查询顺序。Replayer 生成的工作负载可以视为真实工作负载。

**跟踪分析（Trace Analyzing）** 使用收集到的跟踪数据进行重放存在局限性。由于工作负载跟踪可能产生性能开销，因此很难跟踪大规模且持续时间很长的工作负载。此外，跟踪文件的内容对其用户/所有者而言是敏感且机密的，因此 RocksDB 用户很难与其他 RocksDB 开发者或第三方公司的开发者（例如上层应用开发者或存储供应商）共享跟踪数据，以进行基准测试和性能调优。为解决这些局限，我们提出了一种分析 RocksDB 工作负载的方法，根据跟踪中的信息为工作负载建立特征概况。跟踪分析工具读取跟踪文件并提供以下特征：1）每个 CF 中键值对、查询数量和查询类型的详细统计摘要；2）键大小和值大小统计；3）键值对流行度；4）键空间局部性，即将被访问的键与数据库中所有现有键按排序顺序结合起来考察；5）每秒查询数（QPS）统计。

**建模与基准测试（Modeling and Benchmarking）** 我们首先计算任意两个选定变量之间的 Pearson 相关系数，以确保这些变量之间的相关性很低。这样便可以分别对每个变量建模。随后，我们用不同的统计模型拟合所收集的工作负载，以找出拟合误差最低的模型；这种方法比始终使用同一个模型（例如 Zipfian）拟合不同工作负载更加准确。接着，所提出的基准测试便可以基于这些概率模型生成键值查询。详细内容将在第 7 节讨论。

sections.title: 4 General Statistics of Workloads
sections.titleTranslation: 4 工作负载的一般统计特征
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section, we introduce the general workload statistics of each use case including query composition in each CF, KV-pair hotness distributions, and queries per second.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节介绍每个用例的一般工作负载统计特征，包括各 CF 中的查询构成、KV 对热度分布以及每秒查询数。

sections.title: 4.1 Query Composition
sections.originalMarkdown:
By analyzing query composition, we can figure out query intensity, the ratio of query types in different use cases, and the popularity of queries. We find that: 1) Get is the most frequently used query type in UDB and ZippyDB, while Merge dominates the queries in UP2X, and 2) query composition can be very different in different CFs.

**UDB** In this UDB server, over 10.2 billion queries were called during the 14-day period, and there were about 455 million queries called during the last 24 hours. There are six CFs being used in UDB as discussed in 2.2. Although those CFs are stored in the same RocksDB database, the workloads are very different. It is difficult to analyze and model such a mixed workload without the separation of different CFs. The query composition in each CF is shown in Figure 2. Get, Put, and Iterator are three major query types in UDB, especially in Object, Assoc, and Non_SG. Get does not show up in the secondary indexes of objects (Object_2ry) and associations (Assoc_2ry). Object_2ry is built for the purpose of ETL, so Iterator is the major query type. Assoc mostly checks the existence of an association between two objects via Get, while the secondary index (Assoc_2ry) lists the objects that are associated with one target object. Since KV-pairs in Assoc_2ry have no repeating updates, SingleDelete is used in this CF to delete the invalid KV-pairs. In other CFs, regular Delete is called to remove the invalid KV-pairs. Assoc_count stores the number of associations of each object. Therefore, Get and Put are the major query types used in this CF to read and update the counters.

*Figure 2: Distribution of different query types in 14 days.*

**ZippyDB** There is only one CF being used in ZippyDB. Get, Put, Delete, and Iterator_seek (forward Iterator) are the four query types that are used. Over the 24-hour period, there were about 420 million queries called in this shard. The ratios of each query type are: 78% Get, 13% Put, 6% Delete, and 3% Iterator, respectively. Get is the major query type in ZippyDB, which aligns with the read-intensive workload of ObjStorage.

**UP2X** Over the 24-hour period, the RocksDB instance received 111 million queries. Among them, about 92.53% of the queries are Merge, 7.46% of them are Get, and fewer than 0.01% of the queries are Put. The query composition is very different from the UDB and ZippyDB use cases, which are read dominated. About 4.71 million KV-pairs were accessed by Merge, 0.47 million by Get, and 282 by Put. Read-and-modify (Merge) is the major workload pattern in UP2X.
sections.translationMarkdown:
通过分析查询构成，我们可以确定查询强度、不同用例中各查询类型所占的比例以及查询的流行度。我们发现：1）Get 是 UDB 和 ZippyDB 中使用最频繁的查询类型，而 UP2X 中的查询以 Merge 为主；2）不同 CF 的查询构成可能存在很大差异。

**UDB** 在这台 UDB 服务器上，14 天期间调用了超过 102 亿次查询，最后 24 小时内调用了约 4.55 亿次查询。如 2.2 节所述，UDB 使用了六个 CF。尽管这些 CF 存储在同一个 RocksDB 数据库中，但其工作负载差异很大。如果不把不同 CF 分开，就很难分析和建模这种混合工作负载。各 CF 的查询构成如图 2 所示。Get、Put 和 Iterator 是 UDB 中的三种主要查询类型，在 Object、Assoc 和 Non_SG 中尤为如此。对象的二级索引（Object_2ry）和关联的二级索引（Assoc_2ry）中没有 Get。Object_2ry 是为 ETL 而构建的，因此 Iterator 是其主要查询类型。Assoc 主要通过 Get 检查两个对象之间是否存在关联，而二级索引（Assoc_2ry）则列出与某个目标对象关联的对象。由于 Assoc_2ry 中的 KV 对不会被重复更新，因此该 CF 使用 SingleDelete 删除无效 KV 对。在其他 CF 中，则调用常规的 Delete 删除无效 KV 对。Assoc_count 存储每个对象的关联数量。因此，Get 和 Put 是该 CF 中用于读取和更新计数器的主要查询类型。

*图 2：14 天内不同查询类型的分布。*

**ZippyDB** ZippyDB 只使用一个 CF。使用的四种查询类型分别是 Get、Put、Delete 和 Iterator_seek（向前 Iterator）。在 24 小时期间，该分片中调用了约 4.2 亿次查询。各查询类型所占比例分别为：Get 78%、Put 13%、Delete 6%、Iterator 3%。Get 是 ZippyDB 中的主要查询类型，这与 ObjStorage 的读密集型工作负载相符。

**UP2X** 在 24 小时期间，该 RocksDB 实例接收了 1.11 亿次查询。其中，约 92.53% 的查询是 Merge，7.46% 是 Get，少于 0.01% 是 Put。其查询构成与以读取为主的 UDB 和 ZippyDB 用例截然不同。Merge 访问了约 471 万个 KV 对，Get 访问了 47 万个，Put 访问了 282 个。读取并修改（Merge）是 UP2X 的主要工作负载模式。

sections.title: 4.2 KV-Pair Hotness Distribution
sections.originalMarkdown:
To understand the hotness of KV-pairs in each use case, we count how many times each KV-pair was accessed during the 24-hour tracing period and show them in cumulative distribution function (CDF) figures. The X-axis is the access count, and the Y-axis is the cumulative ratio between 0 and 1. We find that in UDB and ZippyDB, most KV-pairs are cold.

**UDB** We plot out the KV-pair access count CDFs for Get and Put. For Iterator, we show the start-key access count distribution and the scan length distribution. The CDFs of Get and Put are shown in Figure 3. Looking at Figure 3(a), more than 70% of the KV-pairs in Assoc are Get requests that occurred at least 2 times. In contrast, this ratio in other CFs is lower than 40%. It indicates that read misses of Assoc happen more frequently than the others. As shown in 3(b), in all CFs, more than 75% of the KV-pairs are Put only one time and fewer than 2% of the KV-pairs are Put more than 10 times. The majority of the KV-pairs are rarely updated.

*Figure 3: The KV-pair access count distribution queried by Get and Put in each CF during 24 hours. (a) The KV-pair access count CDF by Get. (b) The KV-pair access count CDF by Put.*

We plot out the access count CDF of the start-keys of Iterators over the 24-hour period, as shown in Figure 4(a). Most of the start-keys are used only once, which shows a low access locality. Fewer than 1% of the start-keys are used multiple times by Iterators. The scan length of more than 60% of the Iterators is only 1 across all CFs, as shown in Figure 4(b). About 20% of the Iterators in Assoc scan more than 100 consecutive keys, while the ratios for Assoc_2ry and Non_SG are about 10% and 5%, respectively. A very large scan length (higher than 10,000) is very rare, but we can still find some examples of this type in Non_SG and Assoc. The configured range query limit in MySQL creates some special scan lengths. For example, there is a jump at 200 in both Assoc and Non_SG.

*Figure 4: The Iterator scan length and start-key access count CDF of four CFs during 24 hours. (a) The Iterator start-key access count CDF distribution. (b) The Iterator scan length CDF distribution.*

We also count the number of unique keys being accessed in different time periods. As shown in Table 1, during the last 24 hours, fewer than 3% of the keys were accessed. During the 14-day period, the ratio is still lower than 15% for all CFs. In general, most of the keys in RocksDB are “cold” in this use case. On one hand, most read requests are responded to by the upper cache tiers [5,7]. Only the read misses will trigger queries to RocksDB. On the other hand, social media data has a strong temporal locality. People are likely accessing the most recently posted content on Facebook.

*Table 1: The ratios of KV-pairs among all existing KV-pairs being accessed during different time periods in UDB*

| CF name | Object | Object_2ry | Assoc | Assoc_2ry | Assoc_count | Non_SG |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 24 hours | 2.72% | 0.62% | 1.55% | 1.75% | 0.77% | 1.38% |
| 14 days | 14.14% | 6.10% | 13.74% | 10.37% | 14.05% | 11.29% |

**ZippyDB** The average access counts per accessed KV-pair of the four query types (Get, Put, Delete, and Iterator_seek) are: 15.2, 1.7, 1, and 10.9, respectively. Read queries (Get and Iterator_seek) show very good locality, while the majority of the KV-pairs are only Put and Deleted once in the last 24-hour period. The access count distribution is shown in Figure 5. For about 80% of the KV-pairs, Get requests only occur once, and their access counts show a long tail distribution. This indicates that a very small portion of KV-pairs have very large read counts over the 24-hour period. About 1% of the KV-pairs show more than 100 Get requests, and the Gets to these KV-pairs are about 50% of the total Gets that show strong localities. In contrast, about 73% of the KV-pairs are Put only once, and fewer than 0.001% of the KV-pairs are Put more than 10 times. Put does not have as clear a locality as Get does. The CDF of Iterator_seek start-key access counts has a special distribution that we can observe very clearly through the 4 “steps” in the figure. About 55% of the KV-pairs are used as the start-key of Iterator_seek 1 time, 6% of the KV-pairs 11 times, 11% of the KV-pairs 12 times, 5% of the KV-pairs 13 times, 10% of the KV-pairs 23 times, and 10% of the KV-pairs 46 times. The special access count distribution of start-keys is caused by the metadata scanning requests in ObjStorage. For example, if one KV-pair stores the metadata of the first block of a file, it will always be used as the start-key of Iterator_seek when the whole file is requested.

*Figure 5: The KV-pair access count distribution of ZippyDB.*

**UP2X** The CDF distribution of KV-pair access counts is shown in Figure 6. Merge and Get have wide distributions of access counts. If we define a KV-pair accessed 10 times or more during the 24-hour period as a hot KV-pair, about 50% of the KV-pairs accessed by Get and 25% of the KV-pairs accessed by Merge are hot. On the other hand, the ratio of very hot KV-pairs (accessed 100 times or more in the 24-hour period) for Merge is 4%, which is much higher than that of Get (fewer than 1%). Both Merge and Get have a very long tail distribution, as shown in the figure.

*Figure 6: The access count distribution of UP2X.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了了解每个用例中 KV 对的热度，我们统计了 24 小时跟踪期间每个 KV 对被访问的次数，并用累积分布函数（CDF）图展示。X 轴是访问次数，Y 轴是 0 到 1 之间的累积比例。我们发现，在 UDB 和 ZippyDB 中，大多数 KV 对都是冷的。

**UDB** 我们绘制了 Get 和 Put 的 KV 对访问次数 CDF。对于 Iterator，我们展示起始键访问次数分布和扫描长度分布。Get 和 Put 的 CDF 如图 3 所示。从图 3(a) 可以看到，Assoc 中超过 70% 的 KV 对对应发生过至少 2 次的 Get 请求。相比之下，其他 CF 中这一比例低于 40%。这表明 Assoc 的读未命中比其他 CF 更频繁。如图 3(b) 所示，在所有 CF 中，超过 75% 的 KV 对只被 Put 一次，而被 Put 超过 10 次的 KV 对少于 2%。绝大多数 KV 对很少更新。

*图 3：24 小时内各 CF 中由 Get 和 Put 查询的 KV 对访问次数分布。（a）Get 的 KV 对访问次数 CDF。（b）Put 的 KV 对访问次数 CDF。*

我们绘制了 24 小时期间 Iterator 起始键访问次数的 CDF，如图 4(a) 所示。大多数起始键只被使用一次，表现出较低的访问局部性。被 Iterator 多次使用的起始键少于 1%。如图 4(b) 所示，在所有 CF 中，超过 60% 的 Iterator 扫描长度仅为 1。Assoc 中约 20% 的 Iterator 会扫描超过 100 个连续键，而 Assoc_2ry 和 Non_SG 中这一比例分别约为 10% 和 5%。非常大的扫描长度（超过 10,000）十分罕见，但在 Non_SG 和 Assoc 中仍能找到一些此类示例。MySQL 中配置的范围查询限制会产生一些特殊的扫描长度。例如，Assoc 和 Non_SG 都在 200 处出现一次跳跃。

*图 4：24 小时内四个 CF 的 Iterator 扫描长度和起始键访问次数 CDF。（a）Iterator 起始键访问次数 CDF 分布。（b）Iterator 扫描长度 CDF 分布。*

我们还统计了不同时间段内被访问的唯一键数量。如表 1 所示，在最后 24 小时内，被访问的键少于 3%。在 14 天期间，所有 CF 的这一比例仍低于 15%。总体而言，在该用例中，RocksDB 里的大多数键都是“冷”的。一方面，大多数读请求由上层缓存层响应 [5,7]，只有读未命中才会触发对 RocksDB 的查询。另一方面，社交媒体数据具有很强的时间局部性。人们往往会访问 Facebook 上最近发布的内容。

*表 1：UDB 中不同时间段内被访问的 KV 对占全部现有 KV 对的比例*

| CF 名称 | Object | Object_2ry | Assoc | Assoc_2ry | Assoc_count | Non_SG |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 24 小时 | 2.72% | 0.62% | 1.55% | 1.75% | 0.77% | 1.38% |
| 14 天 | 14.14% | 6.10% | 13.74% | 10.37% | 14.05% | 11.29% |

**ZippyDB** 四种查询类型（Get、Put、Delete 和 Iterator_seek）中，每个被访问 KV 对的平均访问次数分别为 15.2、1.7、1 和 10.9。读查询（Get 和 Iterator_seek）表现出很好的局部性，而在最后 24 小时内，大多数 KV 对只被 Put 和 Delete 一次。访问次数分布如图 5 所示。约 80% 的 KV 对只发生一次 Get 请求，其访问次数呈长尾分布。这表明在 24 小时期间，只有很小一部分 KV 对的读取次数非常大。约 1% 的 KV 对发生了超过 100 次 Get 请求，对这些 KV 对的 Get 约占 Get 总数的 50%，表现出很强的局部性。相比之下，约 73% 的 KV 对只被 Put 一次，被 Put 超过 10 次的 KV 对少于 0.001%。Put 的局部性不如 Get 明显。Iterator_seek 起始键访问次数的 CDF 呈现一种特殊分布，可以通过图中的 4 个“阶梯”非常清楚地观察到。约 55% 的 KV 对被用作 Iterator_seek 起始键 1 次，6% 的 KV 对为 11 次，11% 的 KV 对为 12 次，5% 的 KV 对为 13 次，10% 的 KV 对为 23 次，另有 10% 的 KV 对为 46 次。起始键这种特殊的访问次数分布由 ObjStorage 中的元数据扫描请求造成。例如，如果一个 KV 对存储文件第一个数据块的元数据，那么请求整个文件时，它总会被用作 Iterator_seek 的起始键。

*图 5：ZippyDB 的 KV 对访问次数分布。*

**UP2X** KV 对访问次数的 CDF 分布如图 6 所示。Merge 和 Get 的访问次数分布范围很宽。如果将 24 小时期间被访问 10 次或更多次的 KV 对定义为热 KV 对，那么 Get 所访问的 KV 对中约 50% 是热的，Merge 所访问的 KV 对中约 25% 是热的。另一方面，对于 Merge，极热 KV 对（24 小时期间被访问 100 次或更多次）的比例为 4%，远高于 Get 的比例（少于 1%）。如图所示，Merge 和 Get 都呈现非常长的尾部分布。

*图 6：UP2X 的访问次数分布。*

sections.title: 4.3 QPS (Queries Per Second)
sections.originalMarkdown:
The QPS metric shows the intensiveness of the workload variation over time. The QPS of some CFs in UDB have strong diurnal patterns, while we can observe only slight QPS variations during day and night time in ZippyDB and UP2X. The daily QPS variations are related to social network behaviors.

**UDB** The QPS of UDB is shown in Figure 7. Some CFs (e.g., Assoc and Non_SG) and some query types (e.g., Get and Put) have strong diurnal patterns due to the behaviors of Facebook users around the world. As shown in Figure 7(a), the QPS for either Get or Put usually increases from about 8:00 PST and reaches a peak at about 17:00 PST. Then, the QPS quickly drops and reaches its nadir at about 23:00 PST. The QPS of Delete, SingleDelete, and Iterator shows variations, but it is hard to observe any diurnal patterns. These queries are triggered by Facebook internal services, which have low correlation with user behaviors. The QPS of six CFs are shown in Figure 7(b). Assoc and Non_SG have a strong diurnal variation, but the QPS of Non_SG is spikier. Since ETL requests are not triggered by Facebook users, the QPS of Object_2ry is spiky and we cannot find any clear patterns.

**ZippyDB** The QPS of ZippyDB is different from that of UDB. The QPS of ZippyDB varies over the 24-hour period, but we do not find a diurnal variation pattern, especially for Put, Delete, and Iterator_Seek. Since ObjStorage is an object stored at Facebook, object read is related to social network behaviors. Therefore, the QPS of Get is relatively lower at night and higher during the day (based on Pacific Standard Time). Because range queries (Iterator_Seek) are usually not triggered by Facebook users, the QPS for this query type is stable and is between 100 and 120 most of the time.

**UP2X** The QPS of either Get or Put in UP2X does not have a strong diurnal variation pattern. However, the usage of Merge is closely related to the behavior of Facebook users, such as looking at posts, likes, and other actions. Therefore, the QPS of Merge is relatively lower at night (about 1000) and higher during the day (about 1500).

*Figure 7: The QPS variation at different dates and times in a 14-day time span. (a) Overall QPS for each query type at different dates and times in a 14-day time span. (b) Overall QPS of each CF at different dates and times in a 14-day time span.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
QPS 指标反映工作负载随时间变化的剧烈程度。UDB 中某些 CF 的 QPS 具有很强的昼夜模式，而在 ZippyDB 和 UP2X 中，我们只能观察到日间和夜间 QPS 的轻微变化。QPS 的每日变化与社交网络行为有关。

**UDB** UDB 的 QPS 如图 7 所示。受世界各地 Facebook 用户行为的影响，一些 CF（例如 Assoc 和 Non_SG）和一些查询类型（例如 Get 和 Put）具有很强的昼夜模式。如图 7(a) 所示，Get 或 Put 的 QPS 通常从太平洋标准时间约 8:00 开始上升，在约 17:00 达到峰值。随后，QPS 快速下降，并在约 23:00 达到最低点。Delete、SingleDelete 和 Iterator 的 QPS 会发生变化，但很难观察到任何昼夜模式。这些查询由 Facebook 内部服务触发，与用户行为的相关性较低。六个 CF 的 QPS 如图 7(b) 所示。Assoc 和 Non_SG 具有明显的昼夜变化，但 Non_SG 的 QPS 尖峰更多。由于 ETL 请求不是由 Facebook 用户触发的，因此 Object_2ry 的 QPS 呈尖峰状，我们无法发现任何明确模式。

**ZippyDB** ZippyDB 的 QPS 与 UDB 不同。ZippyDB 的 QPS 在 24 小时期间会发生变化，但我们没有发现昼夜变化模式，尤其是在 Put、Delete 和 Iterator_Seek 中。由于 ObjStorage 是 Facebook 上存储对象的服务，对象读取与社交网络行为有关。因此，Get 的 QPS 在夜间相对较低、白天较高（以太平洋标准时间为准）。由于范围查询（Iterator_Seek）通常不是由 Facebook 用户触发的，因此这种查询类型的 QPS 很稳定，大多数时候处于 100 到 120 之间。

**UP2X** UP2X 中 Get 和 Put 的 QPS 都没有明显的昼夜变化模式。然而，Merge 的使用与 Facebook 用户的行为密切相关，例如浏览帖子、点赞以及其他操作。因此，Merge 的 QPS 在夜间相对较低（约为 1000），在白天较高（约为 1500）。

*图 7：14 天时间跨度内不同日期和时刻的 QPS 变化。（a）14 天时间跨度内不同日期和时刻各查询类型的总体 QPS。（b）14 天时间跨度内不同日期和时刻各 CF 的总体 QPS。*

sections.title: 5 Key and Value Sizes
sections.originalMarkdown:
Key size and value size are important factors in understanding the workloads of KV-stores. They are closely related to performance and storage space efficiency. The average (AVG) and standard deviation (SD) of key and value sizes are shown in Table 2, and the CDFs of key and value sizes are shown in Figure 8. In general, key sizes are usually small and have a narrow distribution, and value sizes are closely related to the types of data. The standard deviation of key sizes is relatively small, while the standard deviation of value size is large. The average value size of UDB is larger than the other two.

*Table 2: The average key size (AVG-K), the standard deviation of key size (SD-K), the average value size (AVG-V), and the standard deviation of value size (SD-V) of UDB, ZippyDB, and UP2X (in bytes)*

|  | AVG-K | SD-K | AVG-V | SD-V |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| UDB | 27.1 | 2.6 | 126.7 | 22.1 |
| ZippyDB | 47.9 | 3.7 | 42.9 | 26.1 |
| UP2X | 10.45 | 1.4 | 46.8 | 11.6 |

**UDB** The average key size is between 16 and 30 bytes except for Assoc_2ry, which has an average key size of 64 bytes. The keys in Assoc_2ry consist of the 4-byte MySQL table index, two object IDs, the object type, and other information. Therefore, the key size of Assoc_2ry is usually larger than 50 bytes and has a long tail distribution as shown in Figure 8(a). For other CFs, the keys are composed of the 4-byte MySQL table index as the prefix, and 10 to 30 bytes of primary or secondary keys like object IDs. Thus, the keys show a narrow distribution. Note that the key sizes of a very small number of KV-pairs are larger than 1 KB, which is not shown in the key size CDF due to the X-axis scale limit.

The value size distribution is shown in Figure 8(b). Object and Assoc have a long tail distribution. The value sizes of Object vary from 16 bytes to 10 KB, and more than 20% of the value sizes are larger than 1 KB. The average value size of KV-pairs in Object is about 1 KB and the median is about 235B, which is much larger than those in other CFs. User data, like the metadata of photos, comments, and other posted data, is stored in this CF, which leads to a large value size. In Assoc, the value sizes are relatively small (the average is about 50 bytes) and vary from 10 bytes to 200 bytes.

A very special case is Assoc_count, whose key size and value size are exactly 20 bytes. According to the design of this CF, the key is 20 bytes (bigint association ID) and is composed of a 10-byte counter and 10 bytes of metadata. Since all the information used in secondary index CFs (Assoc_2ry and Object_2ry) is stored in its key, the value does not contain any meaningful data. Therefore, the average value size is less than 8 bytes and there are only three possible value sizes in the distribution (1 byte, 6 bytes, or 16 bytes) as shown in Figure 8(b). For CFs with large value sizes like Object, optimizations like separating key and value [32] can effectively improve performance.

**ZippyDB** Since a key in ZippyDB is composed of ObjStorage metadata, the key sizes are relatively large. The CDF of the key sizes is shown in Figure 8(c). We can find several “steps” in the CDF. Nearly all of the key sizes are in the two size ranges: [48, 53] and [90, 91]. The ratio of KV-pairs in these two key size ranges are different for different query types. For example, about 60% of the key sizes of Get are in the [48, 53] range, while the ratio for Put is about 31%.

The value sizes are collected from Put queries. As shown in Figure 8(d), the value size distribution has a very long tail: about 1% of the value sizes are larger than 400 bytes, and about 0.05% of the value sizes are over 1 KB. Some of the value sizes are even larger than 100 KB. However, most of the KV-pairs have a small value. More than 90% of the value sizes are smaller than 34 bytes, which is even smaller than the key sizes.

**UP2X** The key sizes do not have a wide distribution, as shown in Figure 8(e). More than 99.99% of the KV-pairs accessed by Get have a key size of 9 bytes. About 6% of the KV-pairs inserted by Merge have a key size of 9 bytes, and 94% are 17 bytes. The 17-byte KV-pairs are all cleaned during compaction, and they are never read by upper-layer applications through Get. Put is rarely used in UP2X. Among the 282 KV-pairs inserted by Put, about 8.9% of the key sizes are smaller than 10 bytes, and 47% of them are 46 bytes.

The value size distribution is shown in Figure 8(f). The value sizes of some KV-pairs inserted by Put are extremely large. The average is about 3.6 KB, and about 3% of the KV-pairs are over 100 KB. The value sizes of KV-pairs inserted by Merge have a special distribution. About 40% of the values are smaller than 10 bytes, and about 52% of the values are exactly 64 bytes. A large portion of the updates in UP2X are the counters and other structured information. Thus, the value sizes of those KV-pairs are fixed to 64 bytes.

*Figure 8: The key and value size distributions of UDB, ZippyDB, and UP2X. (a) UDB key size CDF. (b) UDB value size CDF. (c) ZippyDB key size CDF. (d) ZippyDB value size CDF. (e) UP2X key size CDF. (f) UP2X value size CDF.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
键大小和值大小是理解 KV 存储工作负载的重要因素，它们与性能和存储空间效率密切相关。键和值大小的平均值（AVG）与标准差（SD）如表 2 所示，键和值大小的 CDF 如图 8 所示。总体而言，键通常较小且分布范围较窄，而值大小与数据类型密切相关。键大小的标准差相对较小，而值大小的标准差较大。UDB 的平均值大小大于另外两种用例。

*表 2：UDB、ZippyDB 和 UP2X 的平均键大小（AVG-K）、键大小标准差（SD-K）、平均值大小（AVG-V）和值大小标准差（SD-V）（单位：字节）*

|  | AVG-K | SD-K | AVG-V | SD-V |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| UDB | 27.1 | 2.6 | 126.7 | 22.1 |
| ZippyDB | 47.9 | 3.7 | 42.9 | 26.1 |
| UP2X | 10.45 | 1.4 | 46.8 | 11.6 |

**UDB** 除 Assoc_2ry 的平均键大小为 64 字节外，平均键大小介于 16 到 30 字节之间。Assoc_2ry 中的键由 4 字节的 MySQL 表索引、两个对象 ID、对象类型以及其他信息组成。因此，如图 8(a) 所示，Assoc_2ry 的键大小通常超过 50 字节，并呈长尾分布。对于其他 CF，键由作为前缀的 4 字节 MySQL 表索引以及 10 到 30 字节的主键或二级键（如对象 ID）组成。因此，键呈现较窄的分布。需要注意的是，极少数 KV 对的键大小超过 1 KB；由于 X 轴尺度的限制，这些键没有显示在键大小 CDF 中。

值大小分布如图 8(b) 所示。Object 和 Assoc 呈长尾分布。Object 的值大小在 16 字节到 10 KB 之间变化，超过 20% 的值大于 1 KB。Object 中 KV 对的平均值大小约为 1 KB，中位数约为 235B，远大于其他 CF。照片的元数据、评论和其他发布数据等用户数据存储在该 CF 中，因而其值较大。在 Assoc 中，值大小相对较小（平均约为 50 字节），在 10 字节到 200 字节之间变化。

Assoc_count 是一个非常特殊的用例，其键大小和值大小恰好都是 20 字节。根据该 CF 的设计，键为 20 字节（bigint 关联 ID），由一个 10 字节计数器和 10 字节元数据组成。由于二级索引 CF（Assoc_2ry 和 Object_2ry）使用的所有信息都存储在其键中，因此值不包含任何有意义的数据。所以，如图 8(b) 所示，其平均值大小小于 8 字节，分布中只有三种可能的值大小（1 字节、6 字节或 16 字节）。对于 Object 这种值较大的 CF，键值分离 [32] 等优化可以有效提升性能。

**ZippyDB** 由于 ZippyDB 中的键由 ObjStorage 元数据组成，因此键相对较大。键大小的 CDF 如图 8(c) 所示。我们可以在 CDF 中看到几个“阶梯”。几乎所有键大小都落在两个区间内：[48, 53] 和 [90, 91]。对于不同的查询类型，KV 对落在这两个键大小区间内的比例有所不同。例如，Get 约 60% 的键大小处于 [48, 53] 区间，而 Put 的这一比例约为 31%。

值大小采集自 Put 查询。如图 8(d) 所示，值大小分布有非常长的尾部：约 1% 的值大于 400 字节，约 0.05% 的值超过 1 KB。有些值甚至大于 100 KB。不过，大多数 KV 对的值较小。超过 90% 的值小于 34 字节，甚至比键还小。

**UP2X** 如图 8(e) 所示，键大小的分布范围并不宽。Get 所访问的 KV 对中，超过 99.99% 的键大小为 9 字节。Merge 插入的 KV 对中约 6% 的键大小为 9 字节，94% 为 17 字节。这些 17 字节的 KV 对都会在压实期间被清理，而且上层应用永远不会通过 Get 读取它们。UP2X 很少使用 Put。在 Put 插入的 282 个 KV 对中，约 8.9% 的键小于 10 字节，47% 的键为 46 字节。

值大小分布如图 8(f) 所示。Put 插入的一些 KV 对的值非常大，平均约为 3.6 KB，约 3% 的 KV 对超过 100 KB。Merge 插入的 KV 对的值大小呈现一种特殊分布：约 40% 的值小于 10 字节，约 52% 的值恰好为 64 字节。UP2X 中很大一部分更新是计数器和其他结构化信息。因此，这些 KV 对的值大小固定为 64 字节。

*图 8：UDB、ZippyDB 和 UP2X 的键和值大小分布。（a）UDB 键大小 CDF。（b）UDB 值大小 CDF。（c）ZippyDB 键大小 CDF。（d）ZippyDB 值大小 CDF。（e）UP2X 键大小 CDF。（f）UP2X 值大小 CDF。*

sections.title: 6 Key-Space and Temporal Patterns
sections.originalMarkdown:
KV-pairs in RocksDB are sorted and stored in SST files. In order to understand and visualize the key-space localities, we sort all the existing keys in the same order as they are stored in RocksDB and plot out the access count of each KV-pair, which is called the heat-map of the whole key-space. Each existing key is assigned a unique integer as its key-ID, based on its sorting order and starting from 0. We refer to these key-IDs as the key sequence.

The KV-pair accesses show some special temporal patterns. For example, some KV-pairs are intensively accessed during a short period of time. In order to understand the correlation between temporal patterns and key-space locality, we use a time series sequence to visualize these patterns. We sort the keys in ascending order and assign them with key-IDs as previously discussed, and this key sequence is used as the X-axis. The Y-axis shows the time when a query is called. To simplify the Y-axis value, we shift the timestamp of each query to be relative to the tracing start time. Each dot in the time series figure represents a request to a certain key at that time. In the UDB use case, the first 4 bytes of a key are the MySQL table index number due to the key composition of MyRocks. We separate the key-space into different key-ranges that belong to different tables by red vertical lines.

The heat-maps of the three use cases show a strong key-space locality. Hot KV-pairs are closely located in the key-space. The time series figures of Delete and SingleDelete for UDB and Merge for UP2X show strong temporal locality. For some query types, KV-pairs in some key-ranges are intensively accessed during a short period of time.

**UDB** We use the heat-map of Get in Object and Assoc_count over a 24-hour period as an example to show the key-space localities. As shown in Figure 9, hot KV-pairs (with high access counts) are usually located in a small key-range and are close to each other. That is, they show a strong key-space locality (indicated by the dense green areas). Some MySQL tables (the key-ranges between the red vertical lines) are extremely hot (e.g., the green dense area in Object), while other tables have no KV-pair accesses.

*Figure 9: The heat-map of Get in Object and Assoc_count during a 24-hour period. The X-axis represents the key-ID of keys in the whole key-space, and the Y-axis represents the KV-pair access counts. The red vertical lines are the MySQL table boundaries.*

One interesting characteristic is that the KV-pairs with high access counts in Assoc_count are skewed toward the end of the table. In social graphs, new objects are assigned with relatively larger IDs, and new associations are frequently added to the new objects. Therefore, new KV-pairs in Assoc_count are hot and are usually at the end of the MySQL table. Moreover, the heat-maps of Get and Put are similar. Usually, the keys with the most Get queries are the ones with the most Put queries.

Most KV-pairs are deleted only once, and they are unlikely to be reinserted. Therefore, there are no hot KV-pairs in Delete and SingleDelete queries. However, they show some special patterns. For example, some nearby KV-pairs are deleted together in a short period of time as shown in Figure 10.

In Figure 10(a), the deleted KV-pairs in the same table for Object are removed together in a short period of time (indicated by green dots with close Y values). After that, deletions will not happen for a long period of time. Similar patterns also appear in the SingleDelete time series for Assoc_2ry, as shown in Figure 10(b). In some MySQL tables, SingleDelete is intensively called in several short time intervals to remove KV-pairs in the same table. Between any two sets of intensive deletions, SingleDelete is never called, which causes the “green blocks” in the time series figures.

*Figure 10: The time series figure of a 24-hour trace. (a) The time series of Delete queries in Object. (b) The time series of SingleDelete queries in Assoc_2ry.*

In general, KV-pairs are not randomly accessed in the whole key-space. The majority of KV-pairs are not accessed or have low access counts. Only a small portion of KV-pairs are extremely hot. These patterns appear in the whole key-space and also occur in different key-ranges. KV-pairs belonging to the same MySQL table are physically stored together. Some SST files at different levels or data blocks in the same SST file are extremely hot. Thus, the compaction and cache mechanisms can be optimized accordingly.

**ZippyDB** The heat-map of Get in ZippyDB shows a very good key-space locality. For example, as shown in Figure 11, the KV-pairs accessed by Get have high access counts and are concentrated in several key-ranges (e.g., between 1×10^6 and 5×10^6). Hot KV-pairs are not randomly distributed: instead, these KV-pairs are concentrated in several small key-ranges. The hotness of these key-ranges is closely related to cache efficiency and generated storage I/Os. The better a key-space locality is, the higher the RocksDB block cache hit ratio will be. Data blocks that are associated with hot key-ranges will most likely be cached in the RocksDB block cache. These data blocks are actually cold from a storage point of view. With a good key-space locality, the number of data block reads from SST files will be much lower than a random distribution. A similar locality is also found in the Put and Iterator_seek heat-maps. Since all the KV-pairs are deleted once, we did not observe any key-space locality for Delete. In general, the ZippyDB workload is read-intensive and has very good key-space locality.

*Figure 11: Heat-map of KV-pairs accessed by Get in ZippyDB.*

**UP2X** If we look at the heat-map of all KV-pairs accessed by Get as shown in Figure 12, we can find a clear boundary between hot and cold KV-pairs. Note that the whole key-space was collected after the tracing was completed. In the heat-map, the KV-pairs from 0 to about 550,000 are never accessed by Gets, but the KV-pairs from 550,000 to 900,000 are frequently accessed. A similar locality is also shown in the heat-map of Merge. While KV-pairs from 0 to about 550,000 are sometimes accessed by Merge, their average access counts are much lower than those of the KV-pairs from 550,000 to 900,000. This special locality might be caused by a unique behavior of AI/ML services and their data update patterns.

*Figure 12: Heat-map of KV-pairs accessed by Get in UP2X.*

The UP2X use case shows a very strong key-space locality and temporal locality in Merge. However, about 90% of the KV-pairs inserted by Merge are actually cleaned during compaction. Since the key-space heat-map does not show the existence of KV-pairs cleaned by compactions, we plot out the time series sequence for Merge, which can indicate Merge accesses of all KV-pairs. As shown in Figure 13, KV-pairs between 0 and 250,000 are frequently accessed during the 24-hour period. These are KV-pairs between 0 and 900,000 in the whole key-space. The KV-pairs between 250,000 and 4,700,000 show very special key-space and temporal localities. The green blocks indicate that a small range of KV-pairs are intensively called by Merge during half an hour. After that, a new set of KV-pairs (with incrementally composed keys) are intensively accessed by Merge during the next half an hour. These KV-pairs are cleaned during compactions. Get and Put do not have similar temporal and key-space localities.

*Figure 13: The time series of Merge in UP2X.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
RocksDB 中的 KV 对经过排序后存储在 SST 文件中。为了理解并可视化键空间局部性，我们按照所有现有键在 RocksDB 中的存储顺序对其排序，并绘制每个 KV 对的访问次数，将其称为整个键空间的热力图。根据排序次序，从 0 开始为每个现有键分配一个唯一整数作为其键 ID。我们将这些键 ID 称为键序列。

KV 对访问表现出一些特殊的时间模式。例如，一些 KV 对会在短时间内被密集访问。为了理解时间模式与键空间局部性之间的相关性，我们使用时间序列来可视化这些模式。我们按升序排列键，并按照前述方法为其分配键 ID，以该键序列作为 X 轴。Y 轴表示调用查询的时间。为了简化 Y 轴的值，我们将每个查询的时间戳转换为相对于跟踪开始时间的值。时间序列图中的每个点表示在该时刻对某个键的一次请求。在 UDB 用例中，由于 MyRocks 的键构成方式，键的前 4 个字节是 MySQL 表索引号。我们用红色竖线将键空间划分为属于不同表的键范围。

三个用例的热力图都表现出很强的键空间局部性。热 KV 对在键空间中的位置彼此接近。UDB 的 Delete 和 SingleDelete 以及 UP2X 的 Merge 的时间序列图表现出很强的时间局部性。对于某些查询类型，某些键范围内的 KV 对会在短时间内被密集访问。

**UDB** 我们以 24 小时期间 Object 和 Assoc_count 中 Get 的热力图为例，展示键空间局部性。如图 9 所示，热 KV 对（访问次数高）通常位于一个较小的键范围内，并且彼此接近。也就是说，它们表现出很强的键空间局部性（由密集的绿色区域表示）。一些 MySQL 表（红色竖线之间的键范围）非常热（例如 Object 中密集的绿色区域），而另一些表没有任何 KV 对访问。

*图 9：24 小时期间 Object 和 Assoc_count 中 Get 的热力图。X 轴表示整个键空间中键的键 ID，Y 轴表示 KV 对访问次数。红色竖线是 MySQL 表的边界。*

一个有趣的特征是，Assoc_count 中访问次数高的 KV 对向表尾部偏斜。在社交图中，新对象会被分配相对较大的 ID，并且新关联会频繁添加到新对象上。因此，Assoc_count 中的新 KV 对是热的，并且通常位于 MySQL 表的尾部。此外，Get 和 Put 的热力图相似。通常，Get 查询次数最多的键也是 Put 查询次数最多的键。

大多数 KV 对只被删除一次，而且不太可能重新插入。因此，Delete 和 SingleDelete 查询中没有热 KV 对。不过，它们表现出一些特殊模式。例如，如图 10 所示，一些相邻的 KV 对会在短时间内一起被删除。

在图 10(a) 中，Object 同一张表内被删除的 KV 对会在短时间内一起移除（由 Y 值接近的绿色点表示）。此后，很长一段时间内都不会再发生删除。如图 10(b) 所示，Assoc_2ry 的 SingleDelete 时间序列中也出现了类似模式。在某些 MySQL 表中，会在几个较短的时间区间内密集调用 SingleDelete，以删除同一张表中的 KV 对。在任意两组密集删除之间，SingleDelete 从不被调用，因此时间序列图中形成了“绿色块”。

*图 10：24 小时跟踪的时间序列图。（a）Object 中 Delete 查询的时间序列。（b）Assoc_2ry 中 SingleDelete 查询的时间序列。*

总体而言，KV 对并非在整个键空间中随机访问。大多数 KV 对未被访问或访问次数很少，只有一小部分 KV 对极热。这些模式既出现在整个键空间中，也出现在不同的键范围内。属于同一 MySQL 表的 KV 对在物理上存储在一起。不同层级中的某些 SST 文件，或者同一个 SST 文件中的某些数据块非常热。因此，可以据此优化压实和缓存机制。

**ZippyDB** ZippyDB 中 Get 的热力图表现出很好的键空间局部性。例如，如图 11 所示，Get 所访问的 KV 对具有很高的访问次数，并集中在若干键范围内（例如 1×10^6 到 5×10^6 之间）。热 KV 对并非随机分布，而是集中在几个较小的键范围内。这些键范围的热度与缓存效率以及所产生的存储 I/O 密切相关。键空间局部性越好，RocksDB 块缓存的命中率就越高。与热键范围相关的数据块很可能被缓存在 RocksDB 块缓存中。从存储的角度看，这些数据块实际上是冷的。在键空间局部性良好的情况下，从 SST 文件读取数据块的次数将远低于随机分布。在 Put 和 Iterator_seek 的热力图中也发现了类似的局部性。由于所有 KV 对都只被删除一次，我们没有在 Delete 中观察到任何键空间局部性。总体而言，ZippyDB 工作负载是读密集型的，并具有很好的键空间局部性。

*图 11：ZippyDB 中 Get 所访问 KV 对的热力图。*

**UP2X** 如果观察图 12 所示的 Get 所访问全部 KV 对的热力图，可以发现热 KV 对与冷 KV 对之间有一条清晰的边界。需要注意的是，整个键空间是在跟踪完成后采集的。在热力图中，从 0 到约 550,000 的 KV 对从未被 Get 访问，而从 550,000 到 900,000 的 KV 对则被频繁访问。Merge 的热力图中也表现出类似的局部性。尽管从 0 到约 550,000 的 KV 对有时会被 Merge 访问，但其平均访问次数远低于从 550,000 到 900,000 的 KV 对。这种特殊的局部性可能由 AI/ML 服务的独特行为及其数据更新模式造成。

*图 12：UP2X 中 Get 所访问 KV 对的热力图。*

UP2X 用例的 Merge 表现出非常强的键空间局部性和时间局部性。然而，Merge 插入的 KV 对中约 90% 实际上会在压实期间被清理。由于键空间热力图无法显示被压实清理的 KV 对曾经存在，因此我们绘制了 Merge 的时间序列，它能够表示对所有 KV 对的 Merge 访问。如图 13 所示，在 24 小时期间，0 到 250,000 之间的 KV 对被频繁访问。这些是在整个键空间中位于 0 到 900,000 之间的 KV 对。250,000 到 4,700,000 之间的 KV 对表现出非常特殊的键空间局部性和时间局部性。绿色块表示一小段范围内的 KV 对会在半小时内被 Merge 密集调用。此后，一组新的 KV 对（其键以递增方式构成）会在接下来的半小时内被 Merge 密集访问。这些 KV 对会在压实期间被清理。Get 和 Put 不具有类似的时间局部性与键空间局部性。

*图 13：UP2X 中 Merge 的时间序列。*

sections.title: 7 Modeling and Benchmarking
sections.originalMarkdown: After understanding the characteristics of some real-world workloads, we further investigate whether we can use existing benchmarks to model and generate KV workloads that are close to these realistic workloads. We do not consider deletions in our current models.
sections.translationMarkdown: 在了解了一些真实世界工作负载的特征之后，我们进一步研究能否使用现有基准测试对这些工作负载进行建模，并生成接近这些真实工作负载的键值工作负载。当前的模型不考虑删除操作。

sections.title: 7.1 How Good Are the Existing Benchmarks?
sections.titleTranslation: 7.1 现有基准测试的效果如何？
sections.originalMarkdown:
Several studies [6, 26, 47] use YCSB/db_bench + LevelDB/RocksDB to benchmark the storage performance of KV-stores. Researchers usually consider the workloads generated by YCSB to be close to real-world workloads. YCSB can generate queries that have similar statistics for a given query type ratio, KV-pair hotness distribution, and value size distribution as those in realistic workloads. However, it is unclear whether their generated workloads can match the I/Os for underlying storage systems in realistic workloads.

To investigate this, we focus on storage I/O statistics such as block reads, block cache hits, read-bytes, and write-bytes collected by perf_stat and io_stat in RocksDB. To exclude other factors that may influence the storage I/Os, we replay the trace and collect the statistics in a clean server. The benchmarks are also evaluated in the same server to ensure the same setup. To ensure that the RocksDB storage I/Os generated during the replay are the same as those in production, we replay the trace on a snapshot of the same RocksDB in which we collected the traces. The snapshot was made at the time when we started tracing. YCSB is a benchmark for NoSQL applications and ZippyDB is a typical distributed KV-store. Therefore, the workloads generated by YCSB are expected to be close to the workloads of ZippyDB, and we use ZippyDB as an example to investigate. Due to special plugin requirements and the workload complexities of UDB and UP2X, we did not analyze storage statistics for those two use cases.

Before we run YCSB, we set the YCSB parameters of workloada and workloadb to fit ZippyDB workloads as much as possible. That is, we use the same cache size, ensure that the request distribution and scan length follows Zipfian, set the fieldlength as the average value size, and use the same Get/Put/Scan ratios as those shown in Section 4. Since we cannot configure the compression ratio in YCSB to make it the same as ZippyDB, we use the default configuration in YCSB. We normalize the results of the RocksDB storage statistics based on those from the trace replay.

The number of block reads from YCSB is at least 7.7x that of the replay results, and the amount of read-bytes is about 6.2x. The results show an extremely high read amplification. Although the collected amount of write-bytes from YCSB is about 0.74x that of the replay, the actual amount of write-bytes is much lower if we assume YCSB achieves the same compression ratio as ZippyDB (i.e., if the YCSB compression ratio is 4.5, the amount of write-bytes is about 0.41x that of the replay). Moreover, the number of block cache hits is only about 0.17x that of the replay results. This evaluation shows that, even though the overall query statistics (e.g., query number, average value size, and KV-pair access distribution) generated by YCSB are close to those of ZippyDB workloads, the RocksDB storage I/O statistics are actually quite different. db_bench has a similar situation.

Therefore, using the benchmarking results of YCSB as guidance for production might cause some misleading results. For example, the read performance of RocksDB under a production workload will be higher than what we tested using YCSB. The workload of YCSB can easily saturate the storage bandwidth limit due to its extremely high read amplification. Also, the write amplification estimated from the YCSB benchmarking results are lower than in real production. The write performance can be overestimated and might also lead to incorrect SSD lifetime estimates.

With detailed analyses, we find that the main factor that causes this serious read amplification and fewer storage writes is the ignorance of key-space locality. RocksDB reads data blocks (e.g., 16 KB) instead of a KV-pair from storage to memory when it encounters a cache miss. In YCSB, even though the overall KV-pair hotness follows the real-world workload distribution, the hot KV-pairs are actually randomly distributed in the whole key-space. The queries to these hot KV-pairs make a large number of data blocks hot. Due to the cache space limit, a large number of hot data blocks that consist of the requested KV-pairs will not be cached, which triggers an extremely large number of block reads. In contrast, in ZippyDB, hot KV-pairs only appear in some key-ranges, so the number of hot data blocks is much smaller. Similarly, a random distribution of hot KV-pairs causes more updated KV-pairs to be garbage collected in the newer levels during compactions. Therefore, old versions of cold KV-pairs that are being updated are removed earlier in the newer levels, which leads to fewer writes when compacting older levels. In contrast, if only some key-ranges are frequently updated, old versions of cold KV-pairs are continuously compacted to the older levels until they are merged with their updates during compactions. This causes more data to be written during compactions.
sections.translationMarkdown:
一些研究 [6, 26, 47] 使用 YCSB/db_bench + LevelDB/RocksDB 对键值存储的存储性能进行基准测试。研究人员通常认为 YCSB 生成的工作负载接近真实世界的工作负载。对于给定的查询类型比例、键值对热度分布和值大小分布，YCSB 可以生成统计特征与真实工作负载相似的查询。然而，这些生成的工作负载能否与真实工作负载对底层存储系统产生的 I/O 相匹配，尚不明确。

为了研究这一问题，我们重点关注由 RocksDB 中的 perf_stat 和 io_stat 收集的存储 I/O 统计数据，例如块读取次数、块缓存命中次数、读取字节量和写入字节量。为了排除可能影响存储 I/O 的其他因素，我们在一台干净的服务器上回放轨迹并收集统计数据。基准测试也在同一台服务器上进行，以确保使用相同的配置。为了保证回放期间产生的 RocksDB 存储 I/O 与生产环境中的相同，我们在收集轨迹的同一个 RocksDB 实例的快照上回放轨迹。该快照是在开始记录轨迹时创建的。YCSB 是面向 NoSQL 应用的基准测试，而 ZippyDB 是一种典型的分布式键值存储。因此，YCSB 生成的工作负载理应接近 ZippyDB 的工作负载，所以我们以 ZippyDB 为例展开研究。由于 UDB 和 UP2X 存在特殊的插件要求，而且工作负载较为复杂，我们没有分析这两个用例的存储统计数据。

在运行 YCSB 之前，我们设置 workloada 和 workloadb 的 YCSB 参数，使其尽可能拟合 ZippyDB 工作负载。具体而言，我们使用相同的缓存大小，确保请求分布和扫描长度服从 Zipf 分布，将 fieldlength 设置为平均值大小，并采用与第 4 节所示相同的 Get/Put/Scan 比例。由于无法在 YCSB 中配置与 ZippyDB 相同的压缩率，我们采用 YCSB 的默认配置。我们以轨迹回放结果为基准，对 RocksDB 存储统计结果进行归一化。

YCSB 产生的块读取次数至少是回放结果的 7.7 倍，读取字节量约为 6.2 倍。这些结果表明其读放大极高。虽然从 YCSB 收集到的写入字节量约为回放结果的 0.74 倍，但如果假设 YCSB 达到了与 ZippyDB 相同的压缩率，实际写入字节量会低得多（即，如果 YCSB 的压缩率为 4.5，写入字节量约为回放结果的 0.41 倍）。此外，块缓存命中次数仅约为回放结果的 0.17 倍。这一评估表明，尽管 YCSB 生成的总体查询统计数据（例如查询数量、平均值大小和键值对访问分布）接近 ZippyDB 工作负载，但二者的 RocksDB 存储 I/O 统计数据实际上存在很大差异。db_bench 也有类似情况。

因此，使用 YCSB 的基准测试结果指导生产实践可能会产生一些误导性结论。例如，RocksDB 在生产工作负载下的读取性能会高于使用 YCSB 测得的性能。由于读放大极高，YCSB 工作负载很容易使存储带宽达到上限。此外，根据 YCSB 基准测试结果估算的写放大低于实际生产环境中的写放大。这可能导致写入性能被高估，也可能导致对 SSD 寿命的错误估计。

经过详细分析，我们发现，造成严重读放大和存储写入量较少的主要因素，是没有考虑键空间局部性。当发生缓存未命中时，RocksDB 会从存储读取数据块（例如 16 KB）到内存，而不是只读取一个键值对。在 YCSB 中，尽管键值对的总体热度服从真实世界工作负载的分布，但热键值对实际上随机分布在整个键空间中。对这些热键值对的查询会使大量数据块变热。由于缓存空间有限，大量包含所请求键值对的热数据块无法被缓存，从而触发数量极其庞大的块读取。相比之下，在 ZippyDB 中，热键值对只出现在部分键范围内，因此热数据块的数量要少得多。类似地，热键值对的随机分布会使更多被更新的键值对在压缩期间于较新层级中被垃圾回收。因此，正在被更新的冷键值对的旧版本会在较新层级中更早被删除，从而减少压缩较老层级时的写入量。相比之下，如果只有部分键范围被频繁更新，冷键值对的旧版本就会不断被压缩到更老的层级，直至在压缩期间与其更新版本合并。这会导致压缩期间写入更多数据。

sections.title: 7.2 Key-Range Based Modeling
sections.originalMarkdown:
Unlike workloads generated by YCSB, real-world workloads show strong key-space localities according to the workload characteristics presented in Sections 6. Hot KV-pairs are usually concentrated in several key-ranges. Therefore, to better emulate a real-world workload, we propose a key-range based model. The whole key-space is partitioned into several smaller key-ranges. Instead of only modeling the KV-pair accesses based on the whole key-space statistics, we focus on the hotness of those key-ranges.

How to determine the key-range size (the number of KV-pairs in the key-range) is a major challenge of key-range based modeling. If the key-range is extremely large, the hot KV-pairs are still scattered across a very big range. The accesses to these KV-pairs may still trigger a large number of data block reads. If the key-range is very small (e.g., a small number of KV-pairs per range), hot KV-pairs are actually located in different key-ranges, which regresses to the same limitations as a model that does not consider key-ranges. Based on our investigation, when the key-range size is close to the average number of KV-pairs in an SST file, it can preserve the locality in both the data block level and SST level. Therefore, we use average number of KV-pairs per SST file as key-range size.

We first fit the distributions of key sizes, value sizes, and QPS to different mathematical models (e.g., Power, Exponential, Polynomial, Webull, Pareto, and Sine) and select the model that has the minimal fit standard error (FSE). This is also called the root mean squared error. For example, for a collected workload of ZippyDB, the key size is fixed at either 48 or 90 bytes, the value sizes follow a Generalized Pareto Distribution [25], and QPS can be better fit to Cosine or Sine in a 24-hour period with very small amplitude.

Then, based on the KV-pair access counts and their sequence in the whole key-space, the average accesses per KV-pair of each key-range is calculated and fit to the distribution model (e.g., power distribution). This way, when one query is generated, we can calculate the probability of each key-range responding to this query. Inside each key range, we let the KV-pair access count distribution follow the distribution of the whole key-space. This ensures that the distribution of the overall KV-pair access counts satisfies that of a real-world workload. Also, we make sure that hot KV-pairs are allocated closely together. Hot and cold key-ranges can be randomly assigned to the whole key-space, since the locations of key-ranges have low influence on the workload locality.

Based on these models, we further develop a new benchmark using db_bench. When running the benchmark, the QPS model controls the time intervals between two consecutive queries. When a query is issued, the query type is determined by the probability of each query type calculated from the collected workload. Then, the key size and value size are determined by the probability function from the fitted models. Next, based on the access probability of each key-range, we choose one key-range to respond to this query. Finally, according to the distribution of KV-pair access counts, one KV-pair in this key range is selected, and its key is used to compose the query. In this way, the KV queries are generated by the benchmark and follow the expected statistical models. At the same time, it better preserves key-space locality.
sections.translationMarkdown:
根据第 6 节给出的工作负载特征，与 YCSB 生成的工作负载不同，真实世界的工作负载表现出很强的键空间局部性。热键值对通常集中在若干键范围内。因此，为了更好地模拟真实世界的工作负载，我们提出了一种基于键范围的模型。整个键空间被划分为若干较小的键范围。我们不再仅依据整个键空间的统计数据对键值对访问进行建模，而是关注这些键范围的热度。

如何确定键范围大小（键范围内键值对的数量），是基于键范围建模面临的一项主要挑战。如果键范围极大，热键值对仍会分散在一个非常大的范围内，对这些键值对的访问可能仍会触发大量数据块读取。如果键范围非常小（例如每个范围只包含少量键值对），热键值对实际上会位于不同的键范围内，从而退化为与不考虑键范围的模型相同的局限。根据我们的研究，当键范围大小接近一个 SST 文件中的平均键值对数量时，它可以同时保留数据块级和 SST 级的局部性。因此，我们使用每个 SST 文件的平均键值对数量作为键范围大小。

我们首先使用不同的数学模型（例如幂、指数、多项式、Webull、Pareto 和正弦模型）拟合键大小、值大小和 QPS 的分布，并选择拟合标准误差（fit standard error，FSE）最小的模型。该误差也称为均方根误差。例如，对于收集到的一个 ZippyDB 工作负载，键大小固定为 48 或 90 字节，值大小服从广义 Pareto 分布 [25]，而 24 小时周期内的 QPS 可以由振幅很小的余弦或正弦模型更好地拟合。

随后，根据键值对的访问次数以及它们在整个键空间中的顺序，计算每个键范围内每个键值对的平均访问次数，并用分布模型（例如幂分布）进行拟合。这样，在生成一条查询时，我们就能计算由每个键范围响应该查询的概率。在每个键范围内部，我们让键值对访问次数的分布服从整个键空间的分布。这可以确保键值对总体访问次数的分布满足真实世界工作负载的分布，同时也能确保热键值对彼此邻近地分配。由于键范围的位置对工作负载局部性的影响较小，热键范围和冷键范围可以随机分配到整个键空间中。

基于这些模型，我们进一步使用 db_bench 开发了一个新的基准测试。运行该基准测试时，QPS 模型控制相邻两条查询之间的时间间隔。当发出一条查询时，根据从所收集工作负载中计算出的各查询类型概率确定查询类型。然后，根据拟合模型的概率函数确定键大小和值大小。接下来，根据每个键范围的访问概率，选择一个键范围来响应该查询。最后，根据键值对访问次数的分布，在该键范围内选择一个键值对，并使用它的键构造查询。通过这种方式，基准测试生成的键值查询服从预期的统计模型，同时能够更好地保留键空间局部性。

sections.title: 7.3 Comparison of Benchmarking Results
sections.originalMarkdown:
We fit the ZippyDB workload to the proposed model (Delete is excluded) and build a new benchmark called Prefix_dist [20]. To evaluate the effectiveness of key-range-based modeling, we also implement three other benchmarks with different KV-pair allocations: 1) Prefix_random models the key-range hotness, but randomly distributes the hot and cold KV-pairs in each key-range; 2) similar to YCSB, All_random follows the distribution of KV-pair access counts, but randomly distributes the KV-pairs across the whole key-space; and 3) All_dist puts the hot keys together in the whole key-space instead of using a random distribution. All four benchmarks achieve a similar compression ratio as that of ZippyDB.

Similar to the process described in Section 7.1, we configure YCSB workloada and workloadb to fit the ZippyDB workload as closely as possible. We run YCSB with the following 4 different request distributions: 1) uniform (YCSB_uniform), 2) Zipfian (YCSB_zipfian), 3) hotspot (YCSB_hotspot), and 4) exponential (YCSB_exp). We use the same pre-loaded database (with 50 million randomly inserted KV-pairs that have the same average key and value sizes as those of a real-world workload) for the 8 benchmarks. The RocksDB cache size is configured with the same value as the production setup. We run each test 3 times (the following discussion uses average value) and normalize the results based on that of replay.

Figure 14 compares the I/O statistics of the 8 benchmarks. The total number of block reads and the amount of read-bytes by YCSB_zipfian workloads are at least 500% higher than those of the original replay results. Even worse, the number of block reads and the amount of read-bytes of the other three YCSB benchmarking results are even higher, at 1000% or more compared with the replay results. In contrast, the amount of read-bytes of Prefix_dist are only 40% higher, and are the closest to the original replay results. If we compare the 4 benchmarks we implemented, we can conclude that Prefix_dist can better emulate the number of storage reads by considering key-space localities. All_dist and Prefix_random reduce the number of extra reads by gathering the hot KV-pairs in different granularities (whole key-space level vs. key-range level). Note that if YCSB achieves a similar compression ratio, the RocksDB storage I/Os can be about 35-40% lower. However, this is still much worse than the storage I/Os of All_dist, Prefix_random, and Prefix_dist.

*Figure 14: The normalized block read, block cache hit, read-bytes, and write-bytes of benchmarks based on that of the replay. We collected statistics from ZippyDB trace replay results and normalized the statistics from 8 benchmarks. The red line indicates the normalized replay results at 1. The closer the results are to the red line, the better.*

If the same compression ratio is applied, the actual amount of write-bytes by YCSB should be less than 50% of the original replay. Prefix_dist achieves about 60% write-bytes of the original replay. Actually, the mismatch between key/value sizes and KV-pair hotness causes fewer write-bytes compared with the original replay results. In general, YCSB can be further improved by: 1) adding a key-range based distribution model as an option to generate the keys, 2) providing throughput control to simulate the QPS variation, 3) providing key and value size distribution models, and 4) adding the ability to simulate different compression ratios.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们使用提出的模型拟合 ZippyDB 工作负载（不包括 Delete），并构建了一个名为 Prefix_dist 的新基准测试 [20]。为了评估基于键范围建模的有效性，我们还实现了另外三个采用不同键值对分配方式的基准测试：1）Prefix_random 对键范围热度进行建模，但在每个键范围内随机分布热键值对和冷键值对；2）All_random 与 YCSB 类似，服从键值对访问次数的分布，但将键值对随机分布在整个键空间中；3）All_dist 不使用随机分布，而是将整个键空间中的热键集中放置。这四个基准测试均实现了与 ZippyDB 相近的压缩率。

与第 7.1 节所述过程类似，我们配置 YCSB 的 workloada 和 workloadb，使它们尽可能拟合 ZippyDB 工作负载。我们使用以下 4 种不同的请求分布运行 YCSB：1）均匀分布（YCSB_uniform）；2）Zipf 分布（YCSB_zipfian）；3）热点分布（YCSB_hotspot）；4）指数分布（YCSB_exp）。8 个基准测试使用同一个预加载数据库，其中包含 5000 万个随机插入的键值对，其平均键大小和值大小与真实世界工作负载相同。RocksDB 缓存大小被配置为与生产环境相同的值。每项测试运行 3 次（下文讨论采用平均值），并以回放结果为基准对结果进行归一化。

图 14 比较了 8 个基准测试的 I/O 统计数据。YCSB_zipfian 工作负载产生的块读取总次数和读取字节量至少比原始回放结果高 500%。更糟糕的是，另外三个 YCSB 基准测试结果的块读取次数和读取字节量更高，比回放结果高出 1000% 或更多。相比之下，Prefix_dist 的读取字节量仅高出 40%，是最接近原始回放结果的。如果比较我们实现的 4 个基准测试，可以得出结论：通过考虑键空间局部性，Prefix_dist 能够更好地模拟存储读取次数。All_dist 和 Prefix_random 通过以不同粒度聚集热键值对（整个键空间级与键范围级），减少了额外读取次数。请注意，如果 YCSB 达到相近的压缩率，RocksDB 存储 I/O 可以降低约 35%–40%。然而，其结果仍远差于 All_dist、Prefix_random 和 Prefix_dist 的存储 I/O。

*图 14：以回放结果为基准归一化后的各基准测试块读取次数、块缓存命中次数、读取字节量和写入字节量。我们从 ZippyDB 轨迹回放结果中收集统计数据，并对 8 个基准测试的统计数据进行归一化。红线表示归一化后的回放结果，其值为 1。结果越接近红线越好。*

如果采用相同的压缩率，YCSB 的实际写入字节量应小于原始回放结果的 50%。Prefix_dist 的写入字节量约为原始回放结果的 60%。实际上，键/值大小与键值对热度之间的不匹配，使其写入字节量少于原始回放结果。总体而言，可以从以下方面进一步改进 YCSB：1）增加基于键范围的分布模型，作为生成键时的一种可选方式；2）提供吞吐量控制，以模拟 QPS 变化；3）提供键大小和值大小分布模型；4）增加模拟不同压缩率的能力。

sections.title: 7.4 Verification of Benchmarking Statistics
sections.titleTranslation: 7.4 基准测试统计数据的验证
sections.originalMarkdown:
We select the Assoc workload from UDB as another example to verify whether our benchmark can achieve KV query statistics that are very similar to those of real-world workloads. Since 90% of keys are 28 bytes and 10% of keys are 32 bytes in Assoc, we can use these two fixed key sizes. We find that Generalized Pareto Distribution [25] best fits the value sizes and Iterator scan length. The average KV-pair access count of key-ranges can be better fit in a two-term power model [33, 34], and the distribution of KV-pair access counts follows a power-law that can be fit to the simple power model [33, 34]. As we discussed in Section 4.3, because the QPS variation has a strong diurnal pattern, it can be better fit to the Sine model [35] with a 24-hour period.

*Figure 15: The synthetic workload QPS, and the PDF comparisons between the collected workload and the synthetic workload.*
